content.content:
1	use axum_core::extract::{FromRef, FromRequestParts};
2	use http::request::Parts;
3	use std::{
4	    convert::Infallible,
5	    ops::{Deref, DerefMut},
6	};
7	
8	/// Extractor for state.
9	///
10	/// See ["Sharing state with handlers"][sharing-state] for an overview of all approaches to
11	/// sharing state, including when to use `Arc`, how `FromRef` substates work, and what the
12	/// `Router<S>` type parameter means.
13	///
14	/// See ["Accessing state in middleware"][state-from-middleware] for how to
15	/// access state in middleware.
16	///
17	/// State is global and used in every request a router with state receives.
18	/// For accessing data derived from requests, such as authorization data, see [`Extension`].
19	///
20	/// [sharing-state]: crate#sharing-state-with-handlers
21	/// [state-from-middleware]: crate::middleware#accessing-state-in-middleware
22	/// [`Extension`]: crate::Extension
23	///
24	/// # With `Router`
25	///
26	/// ```
27	/// use axum::{Router, routing::get, extract::State};
28	///
29	/// // the application state
30	/// //
31	/// // here you can put configuration, database connection pools, or whatever
32	/// // state you need
33	/// #[derive(Clone)]
34	/// struct AppState {}
35	///
36	/// let state = AppState {};
37	///
38	/// // create a `Router` that holds our state
39	/// let app = Router::new()
40	///     .route("/", get(handler))
41	///     // provide the state so the router can access it
42	///     .with_state(state);
43	///
44	/// async fn handler(
45	///     // access the state via the `State` extractor
46	///     // extracting a state of the wrong type results in a compile error
47	///     State(state): State<AppState>,
48	/// ) {
49	///     // use `state`...
50	/// }
51	/// # let _: axum::Router = app;
52	/// ```
53	///
54	/// Note that `State` is an extractor, so be sure to put it before any body
55	/// extractors, see ["the order of extractors"][order-of-extractors].
56	///
57	/// [order-of-extractors]: crate::extract#the-order-of-extractors
58	///
59	/// ## Combining stateful routers
60	///
61	/// Multiple [`Router`]s can be combined with [`Router::nest`] or [`Router::merge`]
62	/// When combining [`Router`]s with one of these methods, the [`Router`]s must have
63	/// the same state type. Generally, this can be inferred automatically:
64	///
65	/// ```
66	/// use axum::{Router, routing::get, extract::State};
67	///
68	/// #[derive(Clone)]
69	/// struct AppState {}
70	///
71	/// let state = AppState {};
72	///
73	/// // create a `Router` that will be nested within another
74	/// let api = Router::new()
75	///     .route("/posts", get(posts_handler));
76	///
77	/// let app = Router::new()
78	///     .nest("/api", api)
79	///     .with_state(state);
80	///
303:impl<OuterState, InnerState> FromRequestParts<OuterState> for State<InnerState>

1	/// Used to do reference-to-value conversions thus not consuming the input value.
2	///
3	/// This is mainly used with [`State`] to extract "substates" from a reference to main application
4	/// state. See ["Sharing state with handlers"][sharing-state] for a worked example.
5	///
6	/// See [`State`] for more details on how library authors should use this trait.
7	///
8	/// This trait can be derived using `#[derive(FromRef)]`.
9	///
10	/// [`State`]: https://docs.rs/axum/0.8/axum/extract/struct.State.html
11	/// [sharing-state]: https://docs.rs/axum/0.8/axum/index.html#sharing-state-with-handlers
12	// NOTE: This trait is defined in axum-core, even though it is mainly used with `State` which is
13	// defined in axum. That allows crate authors to use it when implementing extractors.
14	pub trait FromRef<T> {
15	    /// Converts to this type from a reference to the input type.
16	    fn from_ref(input: &T) -> Self;
17	}
18	
19	impl<T> FromRef<T> for T
20	where
21	    T: Clone,
22	{
23	    fn from_ref(input: &T) -> Self {
24	        input.clone()
25	    }
26	}
27

content.content:
90	/// Although this future resolves to `io::Result<()>`, it will never actually complete or return an
91	/// error. Errors on the TCP socket will be handled by sleeping for a short while (currently, one
92	/// second).
93	///
94	/// [timeouts]: hyper::server::conn::http1::Builder::header_read_timeout
95	/// [`Router`]: crate::Router
96	/// [`Router::into_make_service_with_connect_info`]: crate::Router::into_make_service_with_connect_info
97	/// [`MethodRouter`]: crate::routing::MethodRouter
98	/// [`MethodRouter::into_make_service_with_connect_info`]: crate::routing::MethodRouter::into_make_service_with_connect_info
99	/// [`Handler`]: crate::handler::Handler
100	/// [`HandlerWithoutStateExt::into_make_service_with_connect_info`]: crate::handler::HandlerWithoutStateExt::into_make_service_with_connect_info
101	/// [`HandlerService::into_make_service_with_connect_info`]: crate::handler::HandlerService::into_make_service_with_connect_info
102	#[cfg(all(feature = "tokio", any(feature = "http1", feature = "http2")))]
103	pub fn serve<L, M, S, B>(listener: L, make_service: M) -> Serve<L, M, S, B, TokioExecutor>
104	where
105	    L: Listener,
106	    M: for<'a> Service<IncomingStream<'a, L>, Error = Infallible, Response = S>,
107	    S: Service<Request, Response = Response<B>, Error = Infallible> + Clone + Send + 'static,
108	    S::Future: Send,
109	    B: HttpBody + Send + 'static,
110	    B::Data: Send,
111	    B::Error: Into<Box<dyn StdError + Send + Sync>>,
112	{
113	    Serve {
114	        listener,
115	        make_service,
116	        executor: TokioExecutor,
117	        _marker: PhantomData,
118	    }
119	}
120	
121	/// A Tokio executor used by [`serve`] to spawn connection tasks, graceful shutdown
122	/// tasks, and hyper's internal tasks (e.g. HTTP/2 connection management).
123	///
124	/// The default executor is [`TokioExecutor`], which simply calls to
125	/// [`tokio::spawn`]. A custom implementation can be provided to wrap
126	/// spawned tasks, e.g. to add tracing or telemetry.
127	///
128	/// Spawned futures rely on Tokio primitives internally, so the executor
129	/// must run them within a Tokio runtime context (e.g. via [`tokio::spawn`]).
130	///
131	/// # Example
132	///
133	/// An executor that wraps every spawned task in a [`tracing`] span.
134	///
135	/// ```
136	/// use std::future::Future;
137	/// use axum::serve::Executor;
138	/// use tokio::task::JoinHandle;
139	/// use tracing::Instrument;
140	///
141	/// #[derive(Clone)]
142	/// struct InstrumentedExecutor;
143	///
144	/// impl Executor for InstrumentedExecutor {
145	///     fn execute<Fut>(&self, fut: Fut) -> JoinHandle<Fut::Output>
146	///     where
147	///         Fut: Future + Send + 'static,
148	///         Fut::Output: Send + 'static,
149	///     {
150	///         let span = tracing::info_span!("axum.serve.task");
151	///         tokio::spawn(fut.instrument(span))
152	///     }
153	/// }
154	/// ```
155	///
156	/// If your executor is expensive to clone, wrap it in an `Arc`.
157	/// A blanket implementation is provided for `Arc<T>` where `T: Executor`.
158	#[cfg(all(feature = "tokio", any(feature = "http1", feature = "http2")))]
159	pub trait Executor: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static {
160	    /// Execute a task.
161	    fn execute<Fut>(&self, fut: Fut) -> JoinHandle<Fut::Output>
162	    where
163	        Fut: Future + Send + 'static,
164	        Fut::Output: Send + 'static;
165	}
166	
167	/// The default executor, which uses [`tokio::spawn`].
168	#[cfg(all(feature = "tokio", any(feature = "http1", feature = "http2")))]
169	#[derive(Clone, Debug)]
170	pub struct TokioExecutor;
171	
172	#[cfg(all(feature = "tokio", any(feature = "http1", feature = "http2")))]
173	impl Executor for TokioExecutor {
174	    fn execute<Fut>(&self, fut: Fut) -> JoinHandle<Fut::Output>
175	    where
176	        Fut: Future + Send + 'static,
177	        Fut::Output: Send + 'static,
178	    {
179	        tokio::spawn(fut)
180	    }
181	}
182	
183	#[cfg(all(feature = "tokio", any(feature = "http1", feature = "http2")))]
184	impl<T> Executor for Arc<T>
185	where
186	    T: Executor,
187	{
188	    fn execute<Fut>(&self, fut: Fut) -> JoinHandle<Fut::Output>
189	    where
190	        Fut: Future + Send + 'static,
191	        Fut::Output: Send + 'static,
192	    {
193	        self.as_ref().execute(fut)
194	    }
195	}
196	
197	/// Future returned by [`serve`].
198	#[cfg(all(feature = "tokio", any(feature = "http1", feature = "http2")))]
199	#[must_use = "futures must be awaited or polled"]
200	pub struct Serve<L, M, S, B, E = TokioExecutor> {
201	    listener: L,
202	    make_service: M,
203	    executor: E,
204	    _marker: PhantomData<fn(B) -> S>,
205	}
206	
207	#[cfg(all(feature = "tokio", any(feature = "http1", feature = "http2")))]
208	impl<L, M, S, B, E> Serve<L, M, S, B, E>
209	where
210	    L: Listener,
211	{
212	    /// Prepares a server to handle graceful shutdown when the provided future completes.
213	    ///
214	    /// # Example
215	    ///
216	    /// ```
217	    /// use axum::{Router, routing::get};
218	    ///
219	    /// # async {
220	    /// let router = Router::new().route("/", get(|| async { "Hello, World!" }));
221	    ///
222	    /// let listener = tokio::net::TcpListener::bind("0.0.0.0:3000").await.unwrap();
223	    /// axum::serve(listener, router)
224	    ///     .with_graceful_shutdown(shutdown_signal())
225	    ///     .await;
226	    /// # };
227	    ///
228	    /// async fn shutdown_signal() {
229	    ///     // ...
230	    /// }
231	    /// ```
232	    ///
233	    /// # Return Value
234	    ///
235	    /// Similarly to [`serve`], although this future resolves to `io::Result<()>`, it will never
236	    /// error. It returns `Ok(())` only after the `signal` future completes.
237	    pub fn with_graceful_shutdown<F>(self, signal: F) -> WithGracefulShutdown<L, M, S, F, B, E>
238	    where
239	        F: Future<Output = ()> + Send + 'static,
240	    {
241	        WithGracefulShutdown {
242	            listener: self.listener,
243	            make_service: self.make_service,
244	            executor: self.executor,
245	            signal,
246	            _marker: PhantomData,
247	        }
248	    }
249	
250	    /// Returns the local address this server is bound to.
251	    pub fn local_addr(&self) -> io::Result<L::Addr> {
252	        self.listener.local_addr()
253	    }
254	
255	    /// Provide a custom [`Executor`] to use for spawning connection tasks and
256	    /// hyper's internal tasks (e.g. HTTP/2).
257	    ///
258	    /// The default is [`TokioExecutor`]. See the [`Executor`] docs for how to
259	    /// implement a custom one.
260	    ///
261	    /// This method can be called before or after [`with_graceful_shutdown`].
262	    ///
263	    /// # Example
264	    ///
265	    /// ```
266	    /// use axum::{Router, routing::get, serve::Executor};
267	    /// # use std::future::Future;
268	    /// # use tokio::task::JoinHandle;
269	    /// #

295	    #[doc = include_str!("../docs/routing/layer.md")]
296	    pub fn layer<L>(self, layer: L) -> Self
297	    where
298	        L: Layer<Route> + Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,
299	        L::Service: Service<Request> + Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,
300	        <L::Service as Service<Request>>::Response: IntoResponse + 'static,
301	        <L::Service as Service<Request>>::Error: Into<Infallible> + 'static,
302	        <L::Service as Service<Request>>::Future: Send + 'static,
303	    {
304	        map_inner!(self, this => RouterInner {
305	            path_router: this.path_router.layer(layer.clone()),
306	            default_fallback: this.default_fallback,
307	            catch_all_fallback: this.catch_all_fallback.map(|route| route.layer(layer)),
308	        })
309	    }
310	
311	    #[doc = include_str!("../docs/routing/route_layer.md")]
312	    #[track_caller]
313	    pub fn route_layer<L>(self, layer: L) -> Self
314	    where
315	        L: Layer<Route> + Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,
316	        L::Service: Service<Request> + Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,
317	        <L::Service as Service<Request>>::Response: IntoResponse + 'static,
318	        <L::Service as Service<Request>>::Error: Into<Infallible> + 'static,
319	        <L::Service as Service<Request>>::Future: Send + 'static,
320	    {
321	        map_inner!(self, this => RouterInner {
322	            path_router: this.path_router.route_layer(layer),
323	            default_fallback: this.default_fallback,
324	            catch_all_fallback: this.catch_all_fallback,
325	        })
326	    }
